refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR uses 'support achieving' which can be interpreted as solution-oriented; refining to 'enable achieving' keeps it solution-neutral and focused on the functional goal. The FR is atomic as it specifies a single functional requirement related to energy efficiency target achievement without combining multiple obligations.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) over the agreed evaluation period and verify it meets or is below the customer-defined energy efficiency target (E_target).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: Implement an energy monitoring and feedback control system to optimize energy consumption dynamically
Design the system with modular energy-efficient components that can be independently optimized
Incorporate adaptive operational modes that adjust system behavior to meet energy targets
Use predictive analytics to forecast energy usage and adjust operations proactively
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement an energy monitoring and feedback control system to optimize energy consumption dynamically

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a control system that can independently monitor and adjust energy use, minimizing coupling with other system functions. This supports modularity and independence in achieving the energy efficiency target.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and timely energy consumption data is available for feedback control
The control system can influence operational parameters sufficiently to impact energy use
Operational policies and safety plans do not restrict dynamic adjustments to system behavior
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces between the energy monitoring/control system and other system modules to ensure clear boundaries and maintain independence.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses energy management and optimization functionalities within the system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive energy consumption data and send feedback control signals.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Enables communication with other system components for data exchange and control coordination.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as energy monitoring and control logically belong here; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Contains physical energy consumption but control logic would diffuse outside.
Not focused on energy management; would cause high diffusion.
Safety related, not energy control; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the energy monitoring and feedback control DP, as it centralizes energy optimization functions and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data may cause some interface complexity.
If energy control requires real-time actuation in Vehicle, some diffusion may occur.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by implementing an energy monitoring and feedback control system to dynamically optimize energy consumption, which directly supports achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target as specified by the acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data may cause interface complexity.
Potential diffusion if real-time actuation is required in the Vehicle module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion with a containment score of 8 and low diffusion score of 2, indicating good containment of the DP. Other modules have higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through telemetry data and network link interfaces that enable measurement of energy consumption and control feedback, allowing validation against the energy_per_ton ≤ E_target acceptance criterion.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, centralizing energy optimization functions and minimizing diffusion across other modules.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface specifications with the Vehicle module to manage dependency and minimize diffusion risk.
Consider defining real-time actuation responsibilities explicitly to avoid diffusion of control logic into the Vehicle module.
Document any assumptions about data latency and control timing between NGHE Solution and Vehicle to ensure performance requirements are met.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirements with a well-defined design parameter and implementation module. The NGHE Solution module shows good containment and low diffusion, and verification feasibility is confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to avoid unnecessary further decomposition that could introduce complexity or diffusion risks. Instead, focus should be on clarifying interfaces and documenting assumptions as recommended.

revise.why: Clarify interface specifications with the Vehicle module to manage dependency and minimize diffusion risk, and explicitly define real-time actuation responsibilities to avoid diffusion of control logic.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can reliably receive raw energy data from the Vehicle module with acceptable latency.
Control feedback loops can be implemented within the NGHE Solution module without requiring real-time actuation in the Vehicle module.
open_risks: Dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data may cause interface complexity.
Potential diffusion if real-time actuation is required in the Vehicle module.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data and network link interface performance metrics to validate energy consumption and control feedback.
Interface specification documents between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules to confirm clarity and containment.
